question: Large holes in Swiss cheese are developed due to production of CO2 by the bacterium ___
question_latex: Large holes in Swiss cheese are developed due to production of $CO_2$ by the bacterium ___
solution: The large holes in Swiss cheese are developed due to production of a large amount of $CO_2$ by a bacterium known as Propionibacterium shermanii.

question: The term 'hartig-net' is related to ........
question_latex: The term 'hartig-net' is related to ........
solution: The hartig-net is a network of hyphae found in ectomycorrhizae, forming between the cells of the root cortex.

question: Match the Column I (Name of Antibiotic) with Column II (Microbial Source) and select the correct option.
question_latex: Match the Column I (Name of Antibiotic) with Column II (Microbial Source) and select the correct option.
solution: Chloromycetin - Streptomyces venezuelae (II), Erythromycin - Streptomyces erythreus (III), Terramycin - Streptomyces aurifaciens (IV), Streptomycin - Streptomyces griseus (I).

question: Match the crop name given in Column-I with respective improved variety of crop developed by mutation breeding given in Column-II. Select the correct option from the following.
question_latex: Match the crop name given in Column-I with respective improved variety of crop developed by mutation breeding given in Column-II. Select the correct option from the following.
solution: Indore-2 → Cotton (IV), Jagannath → Rice (III), Regina-II → Cabbage (I), NP 836 → Wheat (II). These are improved varieties developed by mutational breeding.

question: Match the column I with column II and select the correct option. (Microbial Herbicides and Their Host)
question_latex: Match the column I with column II and select the correct option. (Microbial Herbicides and Their Host)
solution: Phytophthora palmivora - Controls milk weeds in orchards (ii), Alternaria crassa - Controls water hyacinth (iii), Cactoblastis catrorum - Controls cacti weeds (iv), Tyrea moth - Controls the weed Senecio jacobeae (i).

question: Match different types of domestic industries in Column I with their respective products in Column II. Choose the correct option.
question_latex: Match different types of domestic industries in Column I with their respective products in Column II. Choose the correct option.
solution: Apiculture - propolis (iii), Fishery - Isinglass (i), Lac culture - Shellac (ii), Dairy - Khoa (iv). Different methods of culture and products of animals obtained by them.

question: Inland fisheries is referred to:
question_latex: Inland fisheries is referred to:
options: B. Trapping and capturing fishes from sea coast
D. Extraction of oil from fishes
solution: Inland fisheries refers to culturing fish in freshwater bodies such as rivers, lakes, ponds, etc.

question: Which of the following methods is/are used in recovery of healthy plants from diseased plants?
question_latex: Which of the following methods is/are used in recovery of healthy plants from diseased plants?
solution: Meristem culture is used to recover healthy plants from diseased plants as the meristematic cells are generally virus-free.

question: Select correct sequence with respect to the steps of Hybridization technique. I - Hybridization, II - Collection of variable species having desired traits, III - Evaluation and selection of parents, IV - Testing, release and commercialization of new cultivars, V - Selection and testing of superior recombinants
question_latex: Select correct sequence with respect to the steps of Hybridization technique. I - Hybridization, II - Collection of variable species having desired traits, III - Evaluation and selection of parents, IV - Testing, release and commercialization of new cultivars, V - Selection and testing of superior recombinants
solution: Correct sequence: Collection of variable species having desired traits → Evaluation and selection of parents → Hybridization → Selection and testing of superior recombinants → Testing, release and commercialization of new cultivars.

question: A draught breed of cattle is:
question_latex: A draught breed of cattle is:
solution: Malvi is a draught breed of cattle used for pulling heavy loads. Red Sindhi, Gir, and Haryana are milch breeds.

question: Match column I with column II and select the correct option. Column I: Invertase, Pectinase, Lipase, Cellulase. Column II: Trichoderma konigii, Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Sclerotinia libertine, Candida lipolytica.
question_latex: Match column I with column II and select the correct option. Column I: Invertase, Pectinase, Lipase, Cellulase. Column II: Trichoderma konigii, Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Sclerotinia libertine, Candida lipolytica.
solution: Invertase - Saccharomyces cerevisiae (II), Pectinase - Sclerotinia libertine (III), Lipase - Candida lipolytica (IV), Cellulase - Trichoderma konigii (I).

question: Following are all fungal diseases of poultry EXCEPT_____
question_latex: Following are all fungal diseases of poultry EXCEPT_____
solution: Pullorum disease in poultry is caused by Salmonella (a bacterium) causing very high mortality in chickens and is highly infectious. Aspergillosis, Thrush, and Favus are all fungal diseases.

question: Match the antibiotic in column I with its microbial source in column II and select the correct alternative. (i) Chloromycetin, (ii) Erythromycin, (iii) Penicillin, (iv) Streptomycin vs (a) Streptomyces erythreus, (b) Penicillium chrysogenum, (c) Streptomyces venezuelae, (d) Streptomyces griseus
question_latex: Match the antibiotic in column I with its microbial source in column II and select the correct alternative.
solution: Chloromycetin is obtained from Streptomyces venezuelae (c). Erythromycin is obtained from Streptomyces erythreus (a). Penicillin is obtained from Penicillium chrysogenum (b). Streptomycin is obtained from Streptomyces griseus (d).

question: A tool in crop improvement involving identification of genes, transfer and integration is:
question_latex: A tool in crop improvement involving identification of genes, transfer and integration is:
options: A. Protoplast fusion and tissue culture
solution: Genetic engineering involves identification of genes of interest, their transfer into a target organism, and integration into the genome for crop improvement.

question: Explants used for the production of disease free plants is_____
question_latex: Explants used for the production of disease free plants is_____
solution: Apical meristem is used for the production of disease free plants as it is generally free from viral infections.

question: Identify the WRONG statement about micropropagation.
question_latex: Identify the WRONG statement about micropropagation.
options: A. Due to this, multiplication of plants becomes season independent.
B. Rare plants can be conserved by this technique.
C. It is useful for obtaining large number of genetically identical plants.
D. Storage becomes difficult as the micropropagules require large space.
answer: D. Storage becomes difficult as the micropropagules require large space.
solution: Micropropagation is a type of tissue culture where a large number of plantlets can be produced in a small space, very fast within very less time. So storage is easy and requires less space.

question: "Pusa Sadabahar" is resistant to_____ disease.
question_latex: "Pusa Sadabahar" is resistant to_____ disease.
options: A. Leaf curl and chilli mosaic virus
answer: A. Leaf curl and chilli mosaic virus
solution: Pusa Sadabahar is a variety of chilli that is resistant to leaf curl and chilli mosaic virus disease.

question: In Multiple Ovulation Embryo Transfer, cow is administered with _______ to induce follicular superovulation.
question_latex: In Multiple Ovulation Embryo Transfer, cow is administered with _______ to induce follicular superovulation.
solution: In Multiple Ovulation Embryo Transfer (MOET), the cow is administered with Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) to induce follicular superovulation.

question: Exotic breeds:
question_latex: Exotic breeds:
solution: Exotic breeds require a specific environment and are not well adapted to local conditions, unlike indigenous breeds which are hardy and sturdy.

question: In conventional plant breeding, collection of Germplasm means
question_latex: In conventional plant breeding, collection of Germplasm means
options: A. Entire collection of plants/seeds with diverse alleles for all genes of a particular crop.
B. Gene library with genome of different varieties of crop plants.
C. Collection of genes from a particular crop plant.
D. Gene library with collected genes of same variety of crop.
answer: A. Entire collection of plants/seeds with diverse alleles for all genes of a particular crop.
solution: Germplasm collection refers to the entire collection of plants/seeds having diverse alleles for all genes of a particular crop. Conventional breeding is achieved by crossing plants with relevant characters and selecting offsprings with desired combination of characters.

question: Antibiotic Chloromycetin is produced from __
question_latex: Antibiotic Chloromycetin is produced from __
solution: Antibiotic Chloromycetin (Chloramphenicol) is produced from Streptomyces venezuelae.

question: The mycorrhiza in relationship with land plants is a_______
question_latex: The mycorrhiza in relationship with land plants is a_______
solution: Myco means fungus and rhizae means roots of higher plants. Thus mycorrhizae is a symbiotic association between fungi and roots of higher plants.

question: Which of the following is an algal source of SCP?
question_latex: Which of the following is an algal source of SCP?
solution: Chlorella is an algal source of Single Cell Protein (SCP). Candida and Saccharomyces are fungal sources, while Methanobacillus is a bacterial source.

question: Which one of the following disease is caused by bacteria?
question_latex: Which one of the following disease is caused by bacteria?
solution: Black rot of crucifers is caused by bacteria (Xanthomonas campestris). Red rot of sugarcane is caused by fungus, Brown rust of wheat by fungus, and Late blight of potato by oomycete Phytophthora infestans.

question: Match the Column I with the Column II and select the correct option. A-Anabaena, B-Tolypothrix, C-Azotobacter, D-Rhizobium vs I-Beans, II-Azolla, III-Lichens, IV-Cotton
question_latex: Match the Column I with the Column II and select the correct option.
solution: Anabaena - Azolla (II), Tolypothrix - Lichens (III), Azotobacter - Cotton (IV), Rhizobium - Beans (I).

question: Which of the following is NOT a breed of buffalo?
question_latex: Which of the following is NOT a breed of buffalo?
solution: Gir is a breed of cow, not buffalo. Nili, Nagpuri, and Surti are all breeds of buffalo.

question: The part of the plant used in tissue culture is ____
question_latex: The part of the plant used in tissue culture is ____
solution: The part of the plant used in tissue culture is called an explant. Plant tissue culture is based on the principle of Totipotency, which is the inherent ability of a living plant cell to grow, divide, redivide and give rise to a whole plant.

question: The large scale production of Swiss cheese is done by using which of the following microorganisms?
question_latex: The large scale production of Swiss cheese is done by using which of the following microorganisms?
solution: Swiss cheese is produced using the bacterium Propionibacterium shermanii which produces large amounts of $CO_2$ creating the characteristic holes.

question: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: In suspension culture and callus culture technique subculturing is necessary. Statement-II: Subculturing is needed only in suspension culture technique.
question_latex: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: In suspension culture and callus culture technique subculturing is necessary. Statement-II: Subculturing is needed only in suspension culture technique.
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
C. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
D. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
answer: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Both the callus and suspension cultures die in due course of time. Therefore, subculturing is necessary for continuation of both techniques. Hence Statement I is correct and Statement II is incorrect.

question: Which one of the following fungi is NOT used as a mycoherbicide?
question_latex: Which one of the following fungi is NOT used as a mycoherbicide?
solution: Beauveria bassiana is used as a mycoinsecticide (to control insects), not as a mycoherbicide. Fusarium sp., Phytophthora palmivora, and Alternaria crassa are used as mycoherbicides.

question: All of the following are marine fish EXCEPT
question_latex: All of the following are marine fish EXCEPT
solution: Labeo (Rohu) is a freshwater fish. Labeo, Catla, Mrigala, Clarius are some of the fresh water edible fishes consumed in India. Herpedon, Stromateus and Sardinella are marine fishes.

question: Taichung native - the dwarf rice variety in India is a native of:
question_latex: Taichung native - the dwarf rice variety in India is a native of:
solution: Taichung Native-1, a dwarf rice variety, originated from Taiwan but was introduced in India from Japan.

question: Rice variety with five times more ______ has been developed.
question_latex: Rice variety with five times more ______ has been developed.
solution: A rice variety with 5 times more iron has been developed. Genes for Ferritin are isolated from soyabean and Phaseolus and have been transferred to rice.

question: Lac is used in the manufacture of following products EXCEPT
question_latex: Lac is used in the manufacture of following products EXCEPT
solution: Isinglass is obtained from dried swim bladder of bony fishes, not from lac. Lac is used in the manufacture of toys, polish, ink, sealing wax, varnishes, etc.

question: In the secondary treatment of STP the masses of bacteria associated with fungal filaments are called_____
question_latex: In the secondary treatment of STP the masses of bacteria associated with fungal filaments are called_____
solution: In secondary treatment of sewage treatment plant (STP), the masses of bacteria associated with fungal filaments are called flocs. Hyphae and mycelium are fungal components. Flakes are patches or broken away pieces.

question: Removal of suspended, floating, coarse solid particles in sewage treatment is called____
question_latex: Removal of suspended, floating, coarse solid particles in sewage treatment is called____
solution: Removal of suspended, floating, coarse solid particles in sewage treatment is called Preliminary treatment which includes Screening and Grit Chamber.

question: The CO2 content in biogas ranges from______
question_latex: The $CO_2$ content in biogas ranges from______
solution: The $CO_2$ content in biogas ranges from 15-45%. Biogas mainly consists of methane (55-70%) and carbon dioxide (15-45%).

question: Central Silk Research and Training Institute (CSRTI) is located at:
question_latex: Central Silk Research and Training Institute (CSRTI) is located at:
solution: The Central Silk Research and Training Institute (CSRTI) is located at Shanthivials, Mysore.

question: Silkworm spins its cocoon:
question_latex: Silkworm spins its cocoon:
solution: The silkworm spins its cocoon from outside to inside, creating continuous silk filaments around itself.

question: Murrah is a breed of:
question_latex: Murrah is a breed of:
solution: Murrah is a well-known high milk-yielding breed of buffalo found mainly in Haryana, Punjab and Delhi.

question: Identify the correct statement for suspension culture.
question_latex: Identify the correct statement for suspension culture.
options: A. The callus transferred in liquid nutrient medium remains intact even after agitation.
B. Agitation of the medium is not necessary.
C. This does not need sub-culturing at all.
D. This culture grows much faster than callus culture.
answer: D. This culture grows much faster than callus culture.
solution: Suspension culture grows much faster than callus culture. Suspension culture consists of single cells or small groups of cells. Agitation is necessary to keep cells in suspension.

question: Coccidiosis is a disease related to________
question_latex: Coccidiosis is a disease related to________
solution: Coccidiosis is a poultry intestinal protozoan disease. It spreads from one animal to another by infected faecal matter or ingestion of infected tissue.

question: Silk glands of silkworm are modified:
question_latex: Silk glands of silkworm are modified:
solution: The silk glands of silkworm are modified salivary glands that secrete liquid silk protein which hardens on exposure to air.

question: Maximum contribution to the total milk production of our country comes from:
question_latex: Maximum contribution to the total milk production of our country comes from:
solution: In India, buffaloes contribute maximum to the total milk production of the country, producing high-fat milk.

question: New varieties of plants can be produced by:
question_latex: New varieties of plants can be produced by:
options: B. Subjecting them to very heavy dose of radiation
C. Subjecting them to doses of radiation and selection
D. Subjecting them to continuous radiation
solution: New varieties of plants can be produced by selection and hybridization. These are the primary methods used in conventional plant breeding.

question: Main composition of lac is:
question_latex: Main composition of lac is:
options: C. Resin, pigment, wax and glue
answer: C. Resin, pigment, wax and glue
solution: Lac is mainly composed of resin (70-80%), pigment (10%), wax (5-6%), and glue or albuminous matter. It is secreted by the lac insect Laccifer lacca.

question: India contributes_____ % of total lac production in the world.
question_latex: India contributes_____ \% of total lac production in the world.
solution: Lac is an insect product obtained from the abdominal gland of lac insect Laccifer lacca found in tropical and sub-tropical regions on palash, ber and kusum trees. 85% of total lac production takes place in India.

question: Haploid plants are preferred over diploid plants for study of mutation because in haploids:
question_latex: Haploid plants are preferred over diploid plants for study of mutation because in haploids:
solution: In haploid plants, all mutations (both dominant and recessive) express themselves phenotypically since there is only one copy of each gene.

question: Androgenic haploids were produced from anther culture for the first time by:
question_latex: Androgenic haploids were produced from anther culture for the first time by:
solution: Androgenic haploids were produced from anther culture for the first time by Guha and Maheshwari in 1964 using Datura innoxia.

question: Find the CORRECT match from the table with Column A (organism), Column B (genus), Column C (product/category): i. Mackeral - Rastrelliger - Freshwater fish, ii. Honey bee - Apis - Wax, iii. Mirgala - Tacchardia - Marine waterfish, iv. Silkworm - Bombyx - Mulberry silk
question_latex: Find the CORRECT match from the table.
solution: Honey bee - Apis - Wax (ii) is correct. Silkworm - Bombyx - Mulberry silk (iv) is correct. Mackeral (Rastrelliger) is a marine fish, not freshwater fish. Mirgala is a freshwater fish, not marine.

question: The amount of protein per 100 g (without water, approximately 2 eggs) is:
question_latex: The amount of protein per 100 g (without water, approximately 2 eggs) is:
solution: The amount of protein in eggs per 100 g (without water, approximately 2 eggs) is 20.1 g.

question: Lac is a:
question_latex: Lac is a:
solution: Lac is an animal product secreted by the lac insect Laccifer lacca (also called Kerria lacca).

question: In plant breeding, biofortification is a method_______
question_latex: In plant breeding, biofortification is a method_______
options: A. To increase the nutritional value of crop plants.
B. To make the crop plants pest resistance.
C. To improve the yield of the crop plant.
D. To make the crop plants disease resistant.
answer: A. To increase the nutritional value of crop plants.
solution: Biofortification is a method in plant breeding to increase the nutritional value of food crops. This includes biofortification for vitamins, minerals, proteins, and healthy fats.

question: Following are the fungal pathogens to control aphids, mealy bugs and mites EXCEPT ........
question_latex: Following are the fungal pathogens to control aphids, mealy bugs and mites EXCEPT ........
solution: Nosema locustae is a protozoan, not a fungal pathogen. It is used to control grasshoppers. Zoophthora radicans, Beauveria bassiana, and Entomophthora pallidaroseum are all fungal pathogens used to control aphids, mealy bugs and mites.

question: Given below are the steps involved in plant breeding program. Identify correct sequence of these steps. (I) Collection of variability, (II) Hybridization, (III) Evaluation and selection of parents, (IV) Testing, release commercialization of new cultivars, (V) Selection & testing of superior recombinants.
question_latex: Given below are the steps involved in plant breeding program. Identify correct sequence of these steps. (I) Collection of variability, (II) Hybridization, (III) Evaluation and selection of parents, (IV) Testing, release commercialization of new cultivars, (V) Selection & testing of superior recombinants.
solution: Correct sequence: Collection of variability → Evaluation and selection of parents → Hybridization → Selection and testing of superior recombinants → Testing, release commercialization of new cultivars.

question: During secondary treatment of sewage, the masses of bacteria held together by fungal hyphae to form mesh like structures called __
question_latex: During secondary treatment of sewage, the masses of bacteria held together by fungal hyphae to form mesh like structures called __
solution: During secondary treatment of sewage, the masses of bacteria held together by fungal hyphae form mesh like structures called flocks.

question: Which of the following has been recently used for increasing productivity of super milk cows:
question_latex: Which of the following has been recently used for increasing productivity of super milk cows:
options: A. Artificial insemination by a pedigree bull only
B. Superovulation of a high production cow only
D. A combination of superovulation, artificial insemination and embryo transplantation into a carrier cow (surrogate mother)
answer: D. A combination of superovulation, artificial insemination and embryo transplantation into a carrier cow (surrogate mother)
solution: A combination of superovulation, artificial insemination and embryo transplantation into a carrier cow (surrogate mother) is used for increasing the productivity of super milk cows.

question: The draught breeds of cattle include:
question_latex: The draught breeds of cattle include:
solution: Malvi, Nageri and Hallikar are draught breeds of cattle. Ongole and Haryana are dual purpose breeds.

question: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: Diazotrophs are the nitrogen fixing microorganisms, which are exclusively symbiotic. Statement-II: Organic fertilizers include farm yard manure, compost and green manure.
question_latex: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: Diazotrophs are the nitrogen fixing microorganisms, which are exclusively symbiotic. Statement-II: Organic fertilizers include farm yard manure, compost and green manure.
options: A. Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct.
B. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
C. Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
answer: A. Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct.
solution: Nitrogen fixing microorganisms, also called diazotrophs, are of two types: symbiotic and free living. Hence Statement I is incorrect. Organic fertilizers do include farm yard manure, compost and green manure. Hence Statement II is correct.

question: The boll worms attack______
question_latex: The boll worms attack______
solution: The bollworms attack cotton. Indore 2 is the variety of cotton resistant to bollworm. Okra is generally affected by fruit and shoot borer (Pusa Sawani and Pusa E-4). Semi dwarf varieties of rice and water stress resistant varieties of maize have been produced.

question: Given below are two statements with respect to single cell protein. Statement I: Single cell protein refers to the crude, or refined edible protein, extracted from pure microbial cultures or from dead or dried cell biomass. Statement II: SCP can be readily used as fodder for achieving fattening of calves, pigs, in breeding fish and in poultry.
question_latex: Given below are two statements with respect to single cell protein. Statement I: Single cell protein refers to the crude, or refined edible protein, extracted from pure microbial cultures or from dead or dried cell biomass. Statement II: SCP can be readily used as fodder for achieving fattening of calves, pigs, in breeding fish and in poultry.
options: A. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
B. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
C. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
answer: D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Both the statements are correct for Single Cell Proteins (SCP). SCP is extracted from pure microbial cultures and can be used as animal fodder.

question: The world's highly prized wool yielding Pashmina breed is:
question_latex: The world's highly prized wool yielding Pashmina breed is:
solution: Pashmina is a highly prized wool yielding breed of goat (Capra hircus). The Pashmina wool (cashmere) is known for its extreme softness and warmth.

question: Match column I with column II and select the correct options. (a) spinach - (i) High protein content, (b) Bitter gourd - (ii) More Vit A and Minerals, (c) Maize - (iii) More Vit C, (d) Wheat Atlas-66 - (iv) Twice the amount of lysine and tryptophan
question_latex: Match column I with column II and select the correct options.
solution: Spinach - More Vit A and Minerals (ii), Bitter gourd - More Vit C (iii), Maize - Twice the amount of lysine and tryptophan (iv), Wheat Atlas-66 - High protein content (i).

question: Bactrian camel is characterised by:
question_latex: Bactrian camel is characterised by:
options: A. Two humps and long neck
B. Two humps and long limbs
C. Two humps and thick coat
D. Single hump and thick coat
answer: C. Two humps and thick coat
solution: The Bactrian camel (Camelus bactrianus) is characterised by two humps and a thick coat of fur to survive cold climates.

question: Nosema locustae are the ........ which control over the grasshoppers, caterpillars and crickets.
question_latex: Nosema locustae are the ........ which control over the grasshoppers, caterpillars and crickets.
solution: Nosema locustae is a protozoan (microsporidian) used as a biological control agent to control grasshoppers, caterpillars and crickets.

question: Tyrea moth is used as a herbicide, because it controls the growth of
question_latex: Tyrea moth is used as a herbicide, because it controls the growth of
solution: Tyrea moth controls the weed Senecio jacobeae. Many dicot herbs that grow in the field of cereals as weeds can be killed by certain microbes and insects.

question: Best egg layer variety among the following is______
question_latex: Best egg layer variety among the following is______
solution: Leghorn is the best egg laying variety. Kadaknath and Aseel are indigenous varieties. Plymouth Rock is an American breed of domestic chicken.

question: Mycorrhiza forms symbiotic association with_______
question_latex: Mycorrhiza forms symbiotic association with_______
solution: Fungi show symbiotic association with the roots of higher plants termed mycorrhizae, which increase the rate of absorption for roots. They exist either as endomycorrhizae or ectomycorrhizae. Coralloid roots are a symbiotic association between Cycas roots and Anabaena, which helps in $N_2$ fixation.

question: Passive, non-locomotory and non-feeding stage in life history of Silk moth is:
question_latex: Passive, non-locomotory and non-feeding stage in life history of Silk moth is:
solution: The pupa is the passive, non-locomotory, and non-feeding stage in the life history of silk moth. The larva (caterpillar) spins the cocoon around itself and then undergoes metamorphosis into a pupa.

question: In the process of sewage treatment, the activated sludge is digested by____
question_latex: In the process of sewage treatment, the activated sludge is digested by____
solution: In the process of sewage treatment, the activated sludge is digested by anaerobic bacteria in anaerobic sludge digesters, producing biogas (methane + $CO_2$).

question: In poultry, one of the following is best layer.
question_latex: In poultry, one of the following is best layer.
solution: Leghorn is the best egg laying breed in poultry. It is an exotic breed known for high egg production.

question: Honey bees are source of the following EXCEPT
question_latex: Honey bees are source of the following EXCEPT
solution: Isinglass is obtained from dried swim bladder of bony fishes. It is a form of collagen used for clarification of beer or wine. Honey bees are source of honey, wax, bee venom, royal jelly, propolis and bee pollen.

question: Breeding crops for improved nutritional quality is referred to as
question_latex: Breeding crops for improved nutritional quality is referred to as
solution: Biofortification is the process of breeding crops for improved nutritional quality by increasing the content of essential vitamins, minerals, proteins, or healthy fats.

question: Ferritin, an iron protein, is isolated from A and B is transferred to rice for increasing its iron content. Options: (A) Sugar beet - Arabidopsis, (B) Soya bean - Phaseolus, (C) Arabidopsis - Tomato, (D) Canola - Tobacco
question_latex: Ferritin, an iron protein, is isolated from $A$ and $B$ is transferred to rice for increasing its iron content.
solution: Ferritin is found in many animals, plants and bacteria. The genes for ferritin protein isolated from soybean and Phaseolus have been transferred to rice as an example of biofortification transgenic crops that will produce food rich in iron.

question: Which one of the following is incorrect regarding cross-breeding?
question_latex: Which one of the following is incorrect regarding cross-breeding?
options: A. New animal breeds of desirable characters are developed.
C. Hisardale is a product of cross breeding between Bikaneri ewe and Marino rams in Punjab.
D. It involves breeding of superior male of one breed with superior female of another breed.
solution: Cross-breeding helps to increase heterozygosity (not homozygosity). Inbreeding increases homozygosity while cross-breeding increases heterozygosity.

question: The newly selected plants obtained by hybridization are grown in natural fields for at least _____ successive seasons.
question_latex: The newly selected plants obtained by hybridization are grown in natural fields for at least _____ successive seasons.
solution: Newly selected plants produced by hybridization are grown in natural fields for at least 3 successive generations to check the characters and their inheritance and to maintain purity of characters.

question: Which one of the following is a common marine fish?
question_latex: Which one of the following is a common marine fish?
solution: The common marine fishes are Harpadon (Bombay duck), Sardinella (sardine), Rastrelliger (mackerel) and Stromateus (pomphret). Labeo rohita, Catla and Cirrhina mrigala are freshwater fishes.

question: To increase oil content in sunflower and rapeseeds ____ genes are used.
question_latex: To increase oil content in sunflower and rapeseeds ____ genes are used.
options: A. cys E and cys M
solution: To increase oil content in soybean, oil palm, sunflower and rape seeds, Arabidopsis genes are used and it is an example of biofortification.

question: Blue green algae are used as bio fertilizers because they
question_latex: Blue green algae are used as bio fertilizers because they
options: C. Accelerate water and nutrient uptake.
D. Are free living and filamentous.
solution: Blue green algae (BGA) are used as biofertilizer as they contain special cells called heterocysts with the enzyme nitrogenase which helps in fixing atmospheric nitrogen.

question: Using biotechnological application of Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) in agriculture ___ has been designed.
question_latex: Using biotechnological application of Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) in agriculture ___ has been designed.
solution: Bio-insecticidal plants are designed using biotechnological applications of Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) which produces cry proteins toxic to specific insects.

question: Given below are the two statements with respect to Cheese. Statement I: Milk is coagulated with citric acid bacteria and the curd formed is filtered to separate whey. Statement II: The solid mass is then ripened with growth of mold that develops characteristic texture, flavor and taste.
question_latex: Given below are the two statements with respect to Cheese. Statement I: Milk is coagulated with citric acid bacteria and the curd formed is filtered to separate whey. Statement II: The solid mass is then ripened with growth of mold that develops characteristic texture, flavor and taste.
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
B. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
C. Both statements I and statement II are correct.
D. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
answer: B. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
solution: Milk is coagulated with lactic acid bacteria (not citric acid bacteria) and the curd formed is filtered to separate whey. The solid mass is then ripened with growth of mold that develops characteristic texture, flavor and taste. So Statement I is incorrect and Statement II is correct.

question: Aquaculture includes:
question_latex: Aquaculture includes:
solution: Aquaculture includes freshwater fishing, brackish water fishes, and marine fishery. It encompasses all forms of cultivation of aquatic organisms.

question: The length of silk fibre which surrounds a cocoon is about:
question_latex: The length of silk fibre which surrounds a cocoon is about:
solution: The length of silk fibre which surrounds a cocoon is about 800 to 1200 yards (approximately 700-1100 metres).

question: Match causative agent in Column-I with the disease of poultry in Column-II. Select the CORRECT option. (a) Bacteria, (b) Fungus, (c) Virus, (d) Protozoan vs (i) Coccidiosis, (ii) Ranikhet, (iii) Pullorum, (iv) Favus
question_latex: Match causative agent in Column-I with the disease of poultry in Column-II. Select the CORRECT option.
solution: Pullorum is a bacterial disease in poultry (a-iii), Favus is an infectious fungal disease in poultry (b-iv), Ranikhet is a viral disease (c-ii), and Coccidiosis is a protozoan disease in poultry birds (d-i).

question: Himgiri developed by hybridisation and selection for disease resistance against rust pathogens is a variety of:
question_latex: Himgiri developed by hybridisation and selection for disease resistance against rust pathogens is a variety of:
solution: Himgiri is a variety of wheat developed by hybridisation and selection for disease resistance against rust pathogens (leaf and stripe rust).

question: Triticum aestivum is:
question_latex: Triticum aestivum is:
solution: Triticum aestivum (common bread wheat) is hexaploid (2n = 6x = 42). It arose from hybridisation of three different diploid grass species.

question: In tissue culture, single germinating pollen grain will form a plant
question_latex: In tissue culture, single germinating pollen grain will form a plant
solution: In tissue culture (anther culture), a single germinating pollen grain will form a haploid plant since pollen grains contain the haploid (n) number of chromosomes.

question: High levels of Aspartic acid, low nitrogen and sugar content in maize plants prevent the attack by_____
question_latex: High levels of Aspartic acid, low nitrogen and sugar content in maize plants prevent the attack by_____
solution: High levels of aspartic acid, low nitrogen and sugar content in maize plants prevent the attack by stem borers.

question: The initial step in preparation of beer is
question_latex: The initial step in preparation of beer is
solution: The initial step in preparation of beer is malting, in which barley seeds are germinated and then dried. This activates amylase enzymes that convert starch into fermentable sugars.

question: What are the sediments in the settling tanks of tertiary treatment in STP called?
question_latex: What are the sediments in the settling tanks of tertiary treatment in STP called?
solution: Sediments in the settling tanks of tertiary treatment in STP are called activated sludge containing bacteria flocs. A small part of this is passed back to the aeration tank called anaerobic sludge digesters.

question: Bacillus thuringiensis kills the caterpillars by releasing ____ in their guts.
question_latex: Bacillus thuringiensis kills the caterpillars by releasing ____ in their guts.
solution: In the gut of the larvae, the toxin (cry protein) is released from Bacillus thuringiensis and the larvae get killed eventually. The cry proteins are crystalline proteinaceous insecticidal delta-endotoxins.

question: Dr Alexander Fleming discovered antibiotics when he was working with the bacterium____
question_latex: Dr Alexander Fleming discovered antibiotics when he was working with the bacterium____
solution: Dr Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin when he was working with Staphylococcus and noticed that Penicillium notatum inhibited its growth.

question: Which one of following is our indigenous breed of chicken?
question_latex: Which one of following is our indigenous breed of chicken?
solution: Aseel is an indigenous breed of chicken from India known for its strength and fighting ability. Plymouth Rock, White Leghorn, and Rhode Island Red are exotic (foreign) breeds.

question: Which one of the following mollusca groups is primarily used in the pearl formation?
question_latex: Which one of the following mollusca groups is primarily used in the pearl formation?
solution: Pelecypods (bivalves such as oysters - Pinctada) are primarily used in pearl formation. Pearl oysters belong to the class Pelecypoda (Bivalvia).

question: Which among the following is the real product of honey bee?
question_latex: Which among the following is the real product of honey bee?
solution: Propolis (bee glue) is the real product made by honey bees from plant resins. Honey is made from nectar, wax is secreted by the bee, and pollen is collected from flowers.

question: A milch breed of cow is:
question_latex: A milch breed of cow is:
solution: Haryana is a milch (dairy) breed of cow. Malvi and Hallikar are draught breeds. Kankrej is a dual-purpose breed.

question: Which one of the following processes involved in alcohol production is NOT involved in wine production?
question_latex: Which one of the following processes involved in alcohol production is NOT involved in wine production?
solution: Distillation is NOT involved in wine production. Wine is produced by fermentation of grape juice without distillation. Distillation is involved in production of spirits/liquors like whisky, brandy, etc. Malting and mashing are specific to beer production.

question: Lac is used in the
question_latex: Lac is used in the
solution: Lac is used in silvering mirrors. Lac is also used in the manufacture of bangles, toys, gramophone records, varnishes, and sealing wax.

question: Following are Indian buffalo breeds EXCEPT, ____
question_latex: Following are Indian buffalo breeds EXCEPT, ____
solution: Gir and Sindhi are famous Indian cow breeds, not buffalo breeds. Surati, Nili, Murrah, Nagpuri, Mehsana, and Jaffarabadi are all Indian buffalo breeds.

question: Match the Column I containing mutant varieties with Column II containing respective crop plants. (a) Jagannath - (I) Cotton, (b) NP-836 - (II) Cabbage, (c) Indore-2 - (III) Rice, (d) Regina-II - (IV) Wheat
question_latex: Match the Column I containing mutant varieties with Column II containing respective crop plants.
solution: Jagannath → Rice (III), NP-836 → Wheat (IV), Indore-2 → Cotton (I), Regina-II → Cabbage (II). These are mutant varieties of crops produced for food enhancement.
